MEMBER:
BANKING AND COMMERCE COMMITTEE
BONDED INDEBTEDNESS AND
FISCAL AFFAIRS COMMITTEE
ELECTIONS AND REAPPORTIONMENT
COMMITTEE
REVENUE AND TAXATION COMMITTEE
JOINT COMMITTEE ON
CONSTRUCTION AND OPERATIONS
CALIFORNIA PUBLIC PROCUREMENT
ADVISORY BOARD
SELECT COMMITTEE ON TOURISM
AND AVIATION
CHAIRMAN:
DEMOCRATIC CAUCUS
STATE PROCUREMENT AND
EXPENDITURE PRACTICES

PLEASE REPLY TO
SACRAMENTO
ROOM 3086, STATE CAPITOL
SACRAMENTO 95814
TELEPHONE (916) 445-6083

CENTRAL COUNTY
1000 BURNETT AVENUE
SUITE 130
MODESTO 95354
TELEPHONE (415) 689-1973

FRESNO
420 W. THIRD STREET
FRESNO 93650
TELEPHONE (415) 754-3011

WEST COUNTY
2560 MACDONALD AVE.
RICHMOND 94804
TELEPHONE (415) 236-3620

SAN RAMON VALLEY
2880 MITCHELL DRIVE
SUITE 105
SAN RAMON 94583
TELEPHONE (415) 830-2871

Senate

California Legislature

DANIEL E. BOATWRIGHT
CHAIRMAN

Committee on Business and Professions

CHAIRMAN:
SENATE SUBCOMMITTEE
ON SPORTS

REPRESENTING
SEVENTH SENATORIAL DISTRICT
CONTRA COSTA COUNTY

February 22, 1991

Colleen McGee
FPPC Enforcement Division
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Ms. McGee:

     This is to confirm my understanding of our telephone conversation today. As I stated the facts, Senator Boatwright has an interest in real property in Sacramento, a "principal" residence in which he has not resided for three years. The home is jointly owned by he and his wife. As such, this home has not been reported in his annual statements of economic interest. The couple are separated, and a divorce proceeding will be final shortly. His wife continues to reside in the home. He receives no income from his joint ownership of the home.

     On December 28, 1990, Senator Boatwright purchased a new home, and now maintains this as his principal or primary residence.

     My question was whether or not he is required to list either or both of the homes on his statement of economic interest for the year ending December 31, 1990. After looking into the matter, you responded to me that he would not have to report either residence in his statement of economic interest.

     Could you please confirm this in writing at your earliest opportunity, keeping in mind the March 1, 1991 due date for the report?

     Thank you again for your prompt advice on this matter.

Sincerely,

BARRY S. BROKAW
Chief of Staff

BSB/rd

sections.question: My question was whether or not he is required to list either or both of the homes on his statement of economic interest for the year ending December 31, 1990.
sections.question_synthetic: Must Senator Boatwright report either or both of his residences (a principal residence he has not occupied for three years and a newly purchased home as of December 28, 1990) on his statement of economic interest for the year ending December 31, 1990?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request/follow-up letter, not an FPPC advice letter response. The letter references prior telephone advice from FPPC staff (Colleen McGee) stating properties would not require reporting, but requests written confirmation. No formal CONCLUSION section exists as this is the incoming request, not the FPPC's response.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request letter from Senator Boatwright's staff to FPPC Enforcement seeking written confirmation of prior telephone advice regarding whether two residential properties must be reported on the Senator's statement of economic interest for 1990.
